title: Slow Cooked Muffin Feast
description: An earthy and creamy Portuguese dish featuring muffins slow-cooked to perfection, perfect for a festive breakfast or snack.
instructions: Preheat your slow cooker to a low temperature (around 150°F or 65°C).
In a large bowl, mix together the almond milk and freshly chopped thyme leaves.
Add sliced garlic cloves to the mixture. Mix well until the pieces are evenly coated with the liquid.
Place the whole wheat muffins in the slow cooker, leaving enough space between them for air circulation.
Pour the seasoned mixture over the muffins. Make sure all the muffins are fully covered by the milk mixture.
Gently press down on the muffins to ensure they are submerged under the liquid but still slightly visible above it.
Sprinkle a pinch of salt and white pepper evenly over the top. This helps in maintaining moisture and flavor distribution during cooking.
Cover the slow cooker with its lid and cook for 4-6 hours or until all the milk is absorbed by the muffins, resulting in a creamy texture.
Remove from heat after cooking; let it rest for about 10 minutes to allow the flavors to settle. This prevents spilling during serving.
Serve hot as an appetizer or snack with your favorite accompaniments.
